2016–2025 · no race held in 2020

The Block Island Triathlon

Nine seasons of swim, bike, run results — quarter-mile swim, 12-mile bike, 4-mile run. 2,953 finishes, cleaned and explorable below.

Finishers per season

Finish times by year

Median vs. fastest finisher, each season.

Are some years just faster?

The swim and run distances can shift slightly year to year with the tide — the 12-mile bike course doesn't. Comparing the three side by side shows how much of the year-to-year swing is really about the course, not the field getting faster or slower.

Where the clock goes

Average share of total finish time — how long each leg takes, not how much it affects your place (that's the next-but-one section below). Filter to see how the split shifts by year, age, or gender.

Average time by age group

Men and women, every finisher grouped by age.

Where the field actually separates

Swim, bike, and run pace for four finishing cutoffs, each shown as % of the Top 5 group's pace on that same leg (so Top 5 always sits at 100% on every axis — the innermost triangle). The further a leg stretches outward at a looser cutoff, the more that leg is what separates elite from everyone else. Defaults to all nine seasons pooled (top N is always taken within each season first, so a faster or slower course-year can't skew the ranking) — narrow to one season, or by gender, if you're curious.

What actually determines your place

A different question than the time breakdown above. That one showed how long each leg takes; this shows how much each leg actually separates racers from each other. A leg only moves your place if people's times on it differ meaningfully — bike takes the most clock time but varies the least between racers, while run takes less time overall but varies the most, which is why run has the bigger effect on where you finish.

Champions by year

How rankings shift during the race

Each year's top 5 finishers, plus (★) any of the featured racers below who raced that year — rank right after the swim, right after the bike, and at the finish. Lower position = better rank.

Average time, age group by year

Hot (orange) = faster than the fastest cell shown · cool (blue) = slower. Hover or tap a cell for the exact time and sample size.

Faster
Slower
Hover or tap a cell to see the exact time and how many results it's based on.

Age vs. finish time

Every finisher, all years (faint dots), with the average time at each age overlaid as a solid line.

Every division, at a glance

Each bubble is one age group, split by sex. Grouped by age rather than the race's own division codes, since those changed naming conventions for younger age brackets across seasons (e.g. M0014, M1519, and M1824 all really describe the same "under-20" band). Size = number of results recorded. Color = relative rank by average finish time, hot (fastest groups) to cool (slowest) — a few much-slower older age groups won't wash out the smaller differences between everyone else. Tap a bubble for detail.

Tap or click a bubble to see its details.

Featured racers

Full result history for a few specific names, with each leg checked against how the whole field did that same year — since swim and run distance can shift with the tide, a faster raw time doesn't always mean a faster relative performance.

All results

2,953 finishes. Tap a column to sort (desktop). Finish/swim/bike/run are colored green (faster) to red (slower) relative to the current year/gender/age-group filters — searching a name doesn't change the colors, only which rows show.

YearPlaceName AgeSexDiv FinishSwimBikeRun

Source: nine seasons of official Block Island Triathlon results (2020 not held). Relay teams, disqualifications, and incomplete entries were removed during cleaning; see cleaning_notes.md for methodology.
